title: Govt directs Meta not to roll out WhatsApp username feature, seeks explanation in 3 days
content: India's government has halted WhatsApp's new username feature, demanding a detailed explanation within three days. Officials are concerned about impersonation, fraud, and weakened accountability, as users could be misled by similar-looking usernames. Cybersecurity experts and startup founders echo these fears, warning of potential scams and a decline in public trust.

title: China's new rocket launch tech could change the way we go to space
content: China is pioneering an innovative electromagnetic launch system, aiming to catapult rockets to supersonic speeds using electricity before engine ignition. This novel approach, unlike SpaceX's chemical rockets, could significantly reduce fuel needs and launch costs by leveraging ground-based power. Tests are reportedly progressing, with potential for a revolutionary shift in space access if successful.

title: Ukraine takes the war to Moscow: Why Kyiv unleashed its biggest attacks after G7 summit
content: Ukraine has launched its most extensive long-range drone campaign, targeting Russian oil refineries, fuel depots, and military infrastructure deep within its territory. This escalation follows G7 commitments to bolster Ukraine's air defenses and economic resilience. The strikes aim to disrupt Russia's war financing and logistics, with President Putin acknowledging temporary fuel shortages.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi dubbed 'almost unplayable' as England receive major warning
content: England faces a significant challenge if teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debuts against them, according to former spinner Graeme Swann.  Swann described the young batter as "almost unplayable" and noted that few bowlers have found success against him.  While England's bowlers will be tested, Jofra Archer might possess an edge due to his IPL experience with Sooryavanshi.  The highly anticipated debut follows Sooryavanshi's stellar IPL 2026 season.

title: This was being called the 'new gold'. So what went wrong?
content: COMEX Silver has declined 37% since the US-Iran war and is down 52% from its all-time high. In domestic markets. MCX Silver has corrected 20% since the war and 46% from its all-time high.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: Russia turns to India for gasoline as fuel shortages deepen after Ukrainian strikes
content: The Kremlin said on Tuesday that it was in discussions with several countries about importing fuel at acceptable prices. One industry source said India has already shipped at least 60,000 metric tonnes of gasoline to Russia. Another source said two cargoes, each carrying between 30,000 and 40,000 tonnes, have been dispatched.

title: Iran invites Kharge, Khurshid, Khera to Khamenei's burial ceremony
content: Tehran had also invited Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend Khamenei's funeral. However, New Delhi chose Bihar governor Lt Gen Syed Ata Hasnain (Retd) and minister of state for external affairs Pabitra Margherita to represent India at the multi-day funeral, which is set to begin on July 4.

title: Big relief for Sreesanth! Kerala Cricket Association revokes three-year ban
content: Former India pacer S Sreesanth's three-year ban by the Kerala Cricket Association has been revoked. The decision follows Sreesanth's unconditional apology for his critical remarks against the association. This lifts the suspension, allowing him to continue as co-owner of the Aries Kollam Sailors franchise in the Kerala Cricket League. The KCA warned of strict action for any future misconduct.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: 'Give statutory status to NTA': House panel suggests 'NEET in multiple phases' amid paper leak row
content: A Parliamentary panel has recommended conducting NEET-UG in multiple phases across states to ease logistical pressures and suggested granting statutory status to the NTA for stronger examination oversight.  Officials briefed the committee on the NEET re-examination's success and discussed reforms. Concerns were raised about a potential shift to Computer-Based Tests and latecomers being denied entry.

title: US govt lifts export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5; What Anthropic said
content: US government has lifted export controls on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, following national security concerns. The decision, made after weeks of tension and negotiations, allows trusted partners and federal agencies to access the models with new safeguards. Anthropic is restoring access globally, thanking users for their patience and highlighting collaborative efforts to refine AI safety.

title: India-Bangladesh cricket ties back on track? BCB's media rights tender sparks hope
content: India and Bangladesh are poised to resume cricketing ties with a six-match white-ball series in September. The Bangladesh Cricket Board has initiated the process by floating a tender for media rights, signaling a potential thaw in relations after a year of strain. This development follows earlier postponements and diplomatic complexities, with the series tentatively scheduled to begin in early September.

title: Hormuz oil shock sends India back to Russia: Is this a peak or the new normal?
content: Russia has emerged as the dominant supplier of crude oil for India - with imports touching over 240 million barrels in the four months since the US-Iran conflict began. In fact, crude imports from Russia are over 4 times the nearest supplier - UAE at over 58 million barrels.

title: India, Japan to sign LNG security pact during PM Modi-Takaichi bilateral
content: India and Japan are set to sign a crucial agreement to bolster liquefied natural gas (LNG) supply security, aiming to counter global disruptions. A joint task force is anticipated to coordinate stockpiling and enhance energy cooperation. The summit, focusing on economic security and resilient supply chains, will also cover critical minerals, AI, and semiconductors, with significant private sector investments expected.

title: 100+ eminent Indians, Pakistanis urge PM Modi, Sharif to revive bilateral dialogue
content: Among the signatories are former RAW chief A S Dulat, Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha, moderate Hurriyat leader Mirwaiz Umar Farooq, former Union minister Mani Shankar Aiyar, former Pakistan foreign minister Khurshid Mahmud Kasuri, former diplomat Ashraf Jehangir Qazi, along with retired diplomats and members of civil society from both countries.

title: 'Everybody's profiting': Trump reacts to $1.4bn income disclosure
content: US President Trump reported over $1.4 billion in income from cryptocurrency ventures, including his family's World Liberty Financial and a $TRUMP meme coin. He stated he doesn't manage personal finances, attributing profits to the strong stock market. Despite ethics concerns over crypto-friendly policies enacted during his term, the White House denied any conflicts of interest, asserting Trump made the US the 'crypto capital'.

title: Akanksha Chamola vs Ram Kapoor: Is cheating really a ‘mistake’ or a ‘choice’?
content: Akanksha Chamola's strong stance against infidelity on Lock Upp Season 2 ignited a debate: is cheating a mistake or a choice? Experts and psychological research suggest it's a conscious decision, not an accident. Studies reveal powerful individuals may be more prone to affairs, often with little regret, highlighting a complex reality beyond impulsive actions.

title: 'I was not utilised properly': Shardul's explosive claim on Gill-led Eng tour
content: India all-rounder Shardul Thakur expressed his disappointment over being "underutilized" during last year's Test series in England.  He believes he could have made a significant impact had he been given more bowling opportunities, citing a "calculation error" in how he was used. Despite the missed chances, Thakur remains hopeful for future national team call-ups, emphasizing his readiness to contribute.

title: Bengaluru daycare horror: Toddlers put in washing machine, sprayed with toilet jet
content: Five women daycare staff at an IT company campus in Bengaluru face an FIR for allegedly abusing toddlers. Disturbing videos surfaced, showing caregivers threatening and physically mistreating children aged two to three. Allegations include locking children in bathrooms and using a washing machine. Police are questioning the accused and verifying the footage to determine the extent of the abuse.

title: China tightens curbs on foreign investments amid AI, chip tech rivalry with US
content: China has unveiled stringent new regulations to bolster oversight of its overseas investments, particularly in critical tech sectors like AI and semiconductors. These rules, effective Wednesday, aim to safeguard national security and technological capabilities amidst escalating US competition. While designed to enhance outward investment quality, they could complicate global expansion for Chinese firms and access to Chinese capital for foreign partners, raising concerns about technology transfers and broader geopolitical implications.

title: Haaland scores, then dons cowboy gear before unleashing his inner Viking
content: Norway's star striker Erling Haaland celebrated his team's FIFA World Cup Round of 16 qualification with a series of fun social media posts, including cowboy and Viking themes.  His dramatic 86th-minute winner against Ivory Coast secured their spot, making him the fastest to 60 international goals for Norway.  They now face Brazil in the next round.

title: Govt warns of strict action amid fraud concerns over WhatsApp usernames
content: Authorities have issued a stern warning to WhatsApp regarding its new username feature, emphasizing that the platform will be held legally accountable for any cyber fraud or impersonation facilitated by the update.  The government is scrutinizing the feature, which could allow users to adopt names similar to official entities, raising significant concerns about potential misuse.  WhatsApp, however, defends the update as a privacy enhancement, claiming built-in safeguards against abuse.

title: Will Bronny James leave Los Angeles Lakers? Here’s what to know following LeBron James’ exit
content: LeBron James' decision to leave the Los Angeles Lakers has sparked fresh questions about Bronny James' future, but current signs point toward the young guard remaining with the franchise. Bronny's fully guaranteed contract, steady development over his first two NBA seasons and affordable salary make him a valuable depth option as the Lakers transition into a new era built around Luka Dončić and Austin Reaves. While speculation persists about a potential reunion with LeBron on his next team, Los Angeles is under no obligation to facilitate a move, leaving Bronny well positioned to continue carving out his own NBA career.

title: Degrees of denial: Paris blames US for climate change in heated exchange
content: A transatlantic debate ignites as Paris blames America's heavy air conditioning use for contributing to Europe's heatwave deaths. While the US mocks France's lack of AC, experts highlight America's significant emissions. The article contrasts this with the US's own struggles with extreme heat and a dismissive attitude towards climate science, underscoring the urgent need for sustainable cooling solutions.

title: 'We are starting from zero again': Shreyas Iyer reveals Gambhir's message
content: Shreyas Iyer embraces his new role as India's T20I captain, viewing it as a childhood dream realized. Despite a tough start in Ireland, Iyer is focused on instilling a fearless, aggressive brand of cricket. He aims to build a fresh team identity, learning from past experiences and the support staff to forge a relentless and determined unit ready for the challenges ahead.

title: Siya, Chetan 'rehearsed' Ketan's murder on another hill before executing it at Lohagad Fort
content: In a shocking turn of events, Pune Rural police revealed that realtor Ketan Agarwal's fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her boyfriend, Chetan Chaudhary, allegedly rehearsed his murder before pushing him off a cliff at Lohagad Fort. The couple confessed to practicing the act on a hill near Market Yard. Police are planning a scene reconstruction and gait analysis of Chaudhary.

title: 'Carrying water is not degrading': Ashwin addresses criticism over Sooryavanshi
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has defended his stance on young Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's role, emphasizing that carrying drinks and observing from the bench offers valuable learning.  He clarified his earlier comments, stating that such tasks are not demeaning and contribute to a player's understanding of team dynamics.  Despite a stellar IPL, Sooryavanshi's debut is delayed, with Ashwin suggesting patience for the 15-year-old's promising career.

title: Can landlords charge tenants for repainting walls? What Delhi High Court said
content: Tenants aren't always responsible for repainting or minor repairs upon moving out. A Delhi High Court ruling clarified that landlords must distinguish between normal wear and tear, which they cover, and actual damage caused by tenants. While routine painting is the landlord's duty, significant damage like removed fittings can be charged to the tenant, provided the landlord proves it and apportions costs fairly.

title: Stock market: Which are top gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian stock markets surged on Wednesday, with the Sensex and Nifty reclaiming key levels after a two-day slump. Positive global cues, including falling crude oil prices, boosted investor confidence. Sectors like banking and consumer goods saw significant gains, while IT stocks faced pressure. Analysts anticipate a positive second half of the year, citing easing geopolitical tensions and potential trade agreements.

title: Army chief Dhiraj Seth unveils 'VIJAY' vision for future-ready Indian Army
content: General Seth, who took over as the 31st Chief of the Army Staff on Tuesday after General Upendra Dwivedi retired on June 30, addressed reporters after receiving a guard of honour at South Block. Speaking on his first day as Army chief, he described the Indian Army as a force that is fully prepared for combat. He said taking over the Army's top post was both an honour and a responsibility.

title: How Integrated Battle Groups could change the way Indian Army fights future wars
content: The Indian Army has launched a major structural overhaul, creating five Integrated Battle Groups and a Fire Support Group. This move aims to enhance rapid, decisive military responses by integrating infantry, armour, artillery, and logistics under single commanders. The reform, tested under the 17 Mountain Strike Corps, streamlines mobilisation and empowers field commanders for quicker action, building a more agile and lethal future-ready force.

title: Centre issues show cause notice to Delhi Gymkhana Club in eviction proceedings
content: According to the notice, the Club's continued occupation of the land after the government resumed possession for a public purpose amounts to "unauthorized occupation" under Section 2(g) of the Public Premises (Eviction of Unauthorized Occupants) Act, 1971. The government has maintained that the President of India exercised powers under Clause 4 of the perpetual lease deed, which allows the lessor to resume the property if it is required for a public purpose.

title: Bata billed customer Rs 67 over MRP, told to refund and pay compensation
content: Bata India has been held liable by the Kerala consumer commission for overcharging a student Rs 67 above the printed MRP on a pair of shoes. The commission ruled that retailers cannot legally charge more than the MRP unless specific procedures, like clearly marking revised prices on the product and publishing in newspapers, are followed.

title: Do France have the most terrific World Cup attack of all time?
content: On Day 20 of the World Cup, France continued to march in the World Cup like Napolean through Europe. Norway and Halaand are also in the picture while Mexico finally won its 5th game in a World Cup. Read this and more in this edition of Offside.

title: Hardik Pandya trade: CSK enter the race; seven franchises express interest
content: Seven IPL franchises have expressed interest in signing Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder decided to leave Mumbai Indians. Chennai Super Kings (CSK) have joined Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) and Rajasthan Royals in the race, with talks gathering pace. Player consent, leadership opportunities and trade structures are expected to shape one of the biggest IPL moves in recent years.

title: Abhishek Sharma's year-long reign as World No. 1 ends in latest ICC T20I rankings
content: Ishan Kishan has claimed the top spot in the T20I batting rankings, edging out his teammate Abhishek Sharma with a remarkable performance. This is a notable achievement for Kishan, whose recent form, especially in the T20 World Cup, has been impressive. He now joins the ranks of elite Indian batsmen. Both players are preparing for the upcoming England series.

title: Deputy Chief Minister Pawan Kalyan inaugurates 18 ‘Nagara Vanams’ in Andhra Pradesh
content: They are the need of the hour to maintain ecological balance and ensure people have ‘green lung spaces’ where they can breathe fresh air and spend quality time in the lap of nature, he says

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE: Helicopter services suspended amid adverse weather conditions in Kedarnath Dham Yatra
content: District Magistrate of Rudraprayag, Vishal Mishra, said that all helicopter operations for the pilgrimage have been halted until further notice

title: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit becomes IAF Vice-Chief; Army appoints new Vice-Chief and Commanders
content: In the Indian Army, Lieutenant General Sandeep Jain assumed charge as the Vice-Chief of the Army Staff

title: Verification of Gruha Jyothi beneficiaries begins
content: Hubballi Electricity Supply Company has requested electricity consumers to cooperate as door-to-door visits have been launched

title: Will keep fighting against BJP-RSS till last breath: RJD leaders on party’s Foundation Day event
content: Lalu Prasad and Tejashwi Yadav appeal to party members to work towards strengthening the party down to the grassroots level

title: ‘MAVIGUN’ is YSRCP’s election plank in 2029, says Jagan
content: We will project the proposal as an alternative development model and seek people’s mandate as the Amaravati project has become a conduit for corruption, says the YSRCP president

title: Order issued on concessions to staff on Census duty in Kerala
content: KSTA, AKSTU protest government inaction on daily wage appointments in schools in place of teachers on Census duty

title: Idukki reservoir faces critically low storage levels
content: The Idukki hydel power project in Kerala is facing a dire situation, with its reservoir storage plummeting to just 23% as of July 1, a stark decline from 58% a year ago

title: 541 projects to be completed in 100 days, says Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: Speaking in the Assembly after presenting the Kerala Appropriation Bill, 2026, Chief Minister says a countdown has begun for implementing the projects, which have been cleared by the Cabinet

title: Shortage of leukocyte filter sets continue to haunt thalassemia patients in Kozhikode
content: The supply of leukocyte filter sets under the ‘Ashadhara’ scheme was stopped last year after patients complained of adverse reactions

title: Census: enumerators in Kollam visit homes as part of door-to-door data collection
content: To ensure the smooth execution of the process, Kollam district administration has deployed a massive team of 4,911 enumerators and 837 supervisors to coordinate operations on the ground

title: Krishna water released from Prakasam barrage to benefit delta farmers
content: The water will benefit nearly 30 lakh farmers cultivating around 13.08 lakh acres under the Krishna Delta ayacut, enabling them to take up agricultural operations on schedule, says District Collector

title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri gets one-year extension in service
content: Career diplomat Mr. Misri was working as the Deputy National Security Adviser before his appointment as the Foreign Secretary

title: T.N. CM Vijay urges PM Modi to relax, modify certain provisions of VB-G RAM G Act
content: C. Joseph Vijay said the VB-G RAM G scheme, implemented from July 1, imposes an additional financial burden of over ₹5,000 crore under the new sharing pattern and restrictive operational implications on the State Government.

title: Finance Bill passed in Kerala Assembly amid Opposition boycott
content: Bill says the tax revision pertaining to low-alcohol beverages will take effect only after a notification by the government in the official gazette. LDF objects to the Bill on the grounds that UDF government is passing it without referring it to the House’s Subject Committee

title: Large tracts of forest land being retrieved from encroachers in Telangana’s Vikarabad
content: Officials have started to address the rampant deforestation going on for several years under their nose

title: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri gets one-year extension in service
content: The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has approved the extension in service for Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri  in accordance with the provisions of Fundamental Rule 56(d)

title: Revanth stresses revenue mobilisation, realistic budgeting to strengthen State finances
content: Government aiming to generate additional revenue of over ₹35,000 crore without increasing taxes or imposing any fresh burden on the public, primarily by plugging leakages and tightening tax compliance mechanisms

title: Foundation stone for Rayalaseema Steel Plant to be laid on July 3
content: The integrated greenfield steel plant being established by the JSW Group is planned to be developed in two phases, with a total production capacity of 2 MTPA and an overall investment of ₹16,350 crore

title: We’re trying to attract industrial investments in North Karnataka, says Deputy Chief Minister
content: We are offering tax concessions, providing land, water and power supply to those willing to invest outside Bengaluru, according to Parameshwara

title: JPC likely to retain clause on removal of Ministers after 30 days in custody
content: Draft report on 130th Constitution Amendment Bill expected by July 10; panel to finalise recommendations before Monsoon Session

title: ₹100-crore AMRUT 2.0 project launched to boost Penukonda’s water supply
content: After laying the foundation stone for the project, Revenue Minister says the project will provide a lasting solution to Penukonda’s drinking water needs and support the area’s rapid industrial and urban growth

title: Mandya: Only 5% sowing completed as rainfall deficit hits 35%
content: Agriculture Department told to promote alternate crops in view of weak monsoon; one nodal agricultural scientist to be appointed for each of the district’s 30 hoblis to guide farmers

title: NDA should retain power to ensure development of A.P., says CM Naidu
content: Accusing the YSRCP of trying incite conflict among different castes, religions, and regions, the Chief Minister urges people to ensure that the party does not return to power

title: Hunger strike junction at Jantar Mantar
content: The triangle of strikers have a common demand, the resignation of the Union Education Minister; activist Sonam Wangchuk is on the CJP stage; six CPI(ML) students and nine unaffiliated protesters sit on either side

title: Hundreds pay homage to former Manalur MLA P.A. Madhavan
content: Veteran Congress leader is remembered as a tireless organiser. K.C. Venugopl says  P.A. Madhavan had made invaluable contributions to strengthening the Congress organisation and taking its ideals to the people

title: TMC leader Mahua Moitra alleges BJP workers hurled eggs at her in Bengal's Nadia district
content: Mahua Moitra had gone to Palashi in Kaliganj to attend a TMC meeting at MLA Alifa Ahmed's residence when a group of protesters gathered outside and shouted 'go back' slogans

title: As Census begins, enumerators set out to map homes and households
content: The exercise will continue until July 30, after which the Population Enumeration phase of Census 2027 will be undertaken

title: Watch: Jammu and Kashmir should become a ‘bridge of peace’ between India and Pakistan: Mehbooba Mufti
content: Peoples Democratic Party president Mehbooba Mufti on July 1 suggested that the Centre make Jammu and Kashmir a gateway between South and Central Asia, and use its geographical location to strengthen ties with Pakistan and China.

title: Census 2027: Enumerators begin door-to-door visits for Houselisting phase in Kerala
content: More than 61,000 enumerators, mostly teachers and other government employees, have been deployed for the exercise. The State has been divided into houselisting blocks for data collection

title: Three men arrested for bid to bribe TVK MLA for vote during no-confidence motion against T.N. Assembly Speaker
content: Investigation has revealed that V. Ashok Kumar, who is the brother of former DMK Minister and incumbent Coimbatore South MLA V. Senthilbalaji, met the accused, Naresh, in Chennai.

title: Kalaburagi Rotti Global Challenge launched to nurture food start-ups
content: The initiative aims to build a globally recognised food brand around Kalaburagi Rotti, says Assistant Commissioner Sahitya Aladakatti

title: "We Are Half Dead": Gurugram Family Seeks Justice For Son Run Over By Thar
content: Sarthak Mattoo's bike was struck by a speeding Thar in New Delhi's Vasant Kunj and he was left to die.

title: Gujarat Launches 'Health Passport' Scheme For Children Aged Up To 18
content: The statewide initiative was launched on June 27 by the Union Home Minister Amit Shah in the presence of the Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel.

title: Bill To Sack Jailed Ministers Back On Track, Likely In Monsoon Session
content: Under the proposed law, if a minister is accused of an offense punishable by imprisonment of five years or more and remains in custody for 30 days, they will be removed from office - either by the President or Governor, or automatically - on Day 31.

title: "Promise Fulfilled": Bhagwant Mann Launches Financial Aid Scheme For Women
content: Some women at the gathering said they received messages about money being credited to their bank accounts.

title: Man Discovers 'Wife' He Met Online Was Hiding A Secret. Then A Murder
content: The investigation started on June 25, when police found a badly decomposed body wearing women's clothes near the Muscat Phatak railway wall.

title: "Cremated Him With His Cricket Ball": Family Of Boy Killed In Mumbai Tree Collapse
content: As demands for accountability grow, the family and local residents are hoping that the investigation will determine whether prior warnings were ignored and whether civic negligence contributed to the fatal incident.

title: This Forensic Tool Was Used In Gauri Lankesh Case. It May Solve Pune Murder
content: Each person exhibits a repetitive and distinctive gait pattern, making it possible to identify someone from a distance based solely on the way they walk

title: Cosmetics Shop Owner Arrested In Uttarakhand For Supplying Banned Drugs
content: Police said a commercial quantity of Codeine syrup weighing 3.9 kilograms and Tramadol capsules was recovered from his possession.

title: Video: Thief Wanted In 3 States Dangles From Moving Train As Cops Close In
content: Police said Abhijith had escaped from the custody of the Tamil Nadu Police last month while being taken for a medical examination after his arrest in a theft case in Tiruchirappalli.

title: Hyderabad, UAE-Based Firms Partner To Bring Physical Intelligence To India
content: Apart from deploying technology, MEIL and Analog also plan to invest in long-term research, skill development, and building a technology ecosystem in India.

title: Akhilesh Yadav Portrayed As Krishna In Birthday Poster, BJP Demands Apology
content: Around 25-30 workers of the SP Yuvjan Sabha participated in a 'havan' organised in Varanasi to mark his birthday.

title: Bhagwant Mann Goes Big On Cash Doles. Experts Say Sentiments Weigh More
content: Analysts believe welfare schemes alone may not be enough to counter anti-incumbency or emotionally charged political issues in Punjab.

title: Live Wire On Road, 2 Navi Mumbai Students Get Electric Shock, Out Of Danger
content: The Navi Mumbai mayor said they are checking who is ultimately responsible for the situation, whether the line belongs to MSEDCL or another entity

title: Opinion: Opinion | Pakistan Might Just Be Getting A Bit Too Confident - This Time, With Afghanistan
content: For Islamabad, its successes at mediation notwithstanding, toppling the Taliban may turn out to be a far more humbling experience. But then Pakistan, strangely, is known to be suicidal in some ways.

title: Indian Techie, 33, Dies After Car Gets Swept Away In US Flash Floods
content: On Saturday afternoon 33-year-old Venkatesh Doppalapudi was driving to Houston when severe storms had already dropped more than 6 inches of rain in many parts of Kansas. During his journey his vehicle was washed away by the flash floods.

title: "Will Love You Unconditionally": Chinese Firm Sells Hyper-Real Robots
content: The company UBTech says its "U1" robot -- equipped with eye cameras, chest sensors, and listening microphones -- is the world's first full-sized, ultra-realistic humanoid designed for mass production.

title: Bulldozer Action Against Illegal Luxury Bungalows Near Statue Of Unity
content: BJP MLA Darshna Deshmukh Vasava said in 2022, he came to know about such incident through news reports.

title: Surat Civic Body Suspends 5 Over Slum Demolition It Claims It Did Not Order
content: According to Surat Municipal Corporation officials the reason for suspension is to ensure that the departmental investigation can be conducted in a completely fair, neutral, and unbiased manner

title: Lt General Rajesh Pushkar Takes Charge As Southern Command Chief
content: He succeeds Lieutenant General Sandeep Jain, who has taken over as the Vice Chief of the Army Staff, according to a defence release.

title: CPI(M) opposes Centre’s nod for mining in Agency areas
content: It will facilitate private companies’ access to valuable minerals, including gold, beach sand, iron ore and bauxite, under the guise of Ease-of-Doing Business, fears party State secretary Srinivasa Rao

title: IISR signs technology licensing agreements
content: The institute has licensed the State Kudumbashree Mission to multiply and disseminate improved varieties of black pepper, turmeric, ginger, cardamom, and cinnamon

title: Rs 35cr bribe to topple Vijay govt? TVK, DMK trade MLA poaching charges
content: the ruling TVK and opposition DMK accused each other of trying to poach their MLAs . televisionK linked the "attempt to topple TVK govt" to former minister V Senthil Balaji . the police arrested three people including Thirunavukkarasu, accusing them of attempting to induce televisionK .
